channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: There's now an OpenClaw app for iOS and Android phones
item.description: Smartphones are welcoming the agentic AI overlords.

item.title: How to watch Netherlands vs Morocco: Free Streams, TV Channels & Kick-Off time for FIFA World Cup 2026 in a crunch last-32 encounter
item.description: Here's how to watch Netherlands vs Morocco for free online and from anywhere at the FIFA World Cup 2026, in an entertaining last-32 tie between two dark horses.

item.title: Quote of the day by Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates: 'If a robot comes in to do the same thing, you’d think that we’d tax the robot' — devising policy ideas to counter automation risks
item.description: Slowing down the robotics, AI, and automation build-out might give us time to understand how to structure our labor markets

item.title: Meta Contractors Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
item.description: Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.

item.title: Comment bien choisir sa caméra de surveillance en 2026 ?
item.description: Il est facile d'installer une caméra de surveillance qui se connecte en Wi-Fi pour envoyer les images sur votre téléphone. Voici comment choisir votre caméra de surveillance en 2026.

item.title: Why quantum computing may be the White Houses new AI
item.description: Quantum computing is the tech world's latest obsession. Here's what it actually is, how it works

item.title: T-Mobile is booting customers from its oldest plans
item.description: Earlier today, T-Mobile started notifying customers that it will be retiring many legacy plans and moving subscribers onto one of its current rate plans. This move includes plans that date back to the 3G era, and it's going about as well as you'd expect. Affected customers began sharing screenshots of the text on reddit and […]

item.title: Buying a Mattress in 2026? We Tested 100+ and These Were the Standouts
item.description: WIRED has tested 100-plus bed-in-a-box mattresses for a week each. Our top pick, the Helix Midnight Luxe hybrid, is the best bed you can buy online.

item.title: 'Near zero-cost memory expansion through recycling': Meta will reuse terabytes worth of DDR4 memory using CXL tech and avoid paying the RAM tax
item.description: Meta reused retired DDR4 memory through CXL technology, reducing server requirements and avoiding expensive new DRAM purchases.

item.title: I went inside FIFA's super-secret Technology Command Center for the World Cup — but sadly I can't really tell you too much about what I saw
item.description: The FIFA World Cup Technology Command Center was a tech nerd's dream - but you'll just have to believe me.

item.title: After a great start, DC’s new cinematic universe is already slowing down
item.description: While Kara Zor-El's appearance at the end of James Gunn's Superman was a very pleasant surprise, Warner Bros. Discovery's plan to fast-track a standalone Supergirl feature always felt a little dubious. It seemed odd that, after Superman, the studio wanted to flesh out its new cinematic universe with films about another Kryptonian and one of […]

item.title: Chamath Palihapitiya raises $135M Series A for his AI coding startup, takes CEO role
item.description: VCs remain thirsty to fund AI coding startups. This one, founded by investor Chamath Palihapitiya, is no exception.

item.title: Reserve your WhatsApp username before its too late
item.description: WhatsApp is now letting its more than 3 billion users reserve the username they'd like to use on the platform.

item.title: The Wassabi Brothers say creator success isnt about subscribers anymore
item.description: The Wassabi Brothers discuss adapting to new platforms, building community over chasing metrics, and what today's creators can learn from YouTube's early days.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 could have a record-breakingly bright display
item.description: Samsung's Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 reportedly has a display that will make the smartwatch the brightest in the business.

item.title: Uber is no longer offering Waymo rides in Phoenix
item.description: Rather than external partnerships, Uber may start leaning on its own robotaxis for driverless options.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup in Netherlands – can I get a free stream?
item.description: Here's how to watch the FIFA World Cup 2026 in the Netherlands for free online and from anywhere in the world, as Ronald Koeman's Oranje go for glory.

item.title: DeepSeek open sources DSpark, a new framework to speed up LLM inference by up to 85%
item.description: Even as the geopolitical conversation around AI continues to grow more fraught following the U.S. government's actions to limit the new models from Anthropic and OpenAI, Chinese open source darling DeepSeek is back with yet another open release that could once again change AI development around the globe. Over the weekend, the firm released DSpark, a new, MIT-Licensed system designed to make large language models answer faster without changing what the underlying model is trying to say. The easiest way to think about it is this: most AI chatbots write like someone crossing a river one stepping...

item.title: SCOTUS: Feds need a warrant to scoop up your phones location data
item.description: Privacy advocates herald new limits on "geofence" searches that track all phones at a crime scene.

item.title: Sidney Raskind tells Mashable the story of his 15-year journey to TikTok success
item.description: Sidney Raskind talked to Mashable at VidCon 2026 as part of the Creator Playbook series, sharing the highs and lows of his content creation journey.

item.title: Leaked iPhone 18 Pro photos reportedly wound up on the dark web
item.description: Leaked iPhone 18 Pro photos and parts lists appeared on the dark web following a data breach affecting one of Apple's key suppliers, according to a report from Reuters. The leaked images show a drop test of what a source tells Reuters is the iPhone 18 Pro, equipped with a three-camera layout and Apple logo. […]

item.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
item.description: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.

item.title: The top Android 17 features to get excited about
item.description: Android 17 is officially here, and we've put together a list of the coolest features to look forward to as you install it.
